title: Fatal: stale `MemoryError` trips `_Py_CheckSlotResult` in `reload_singlephase_extension` (`import.c:2011`)
description: `Python/import.c`: in `reload_singlephase_extension`, when `_modules_by_index_set()` (L2010) fails under OOM (its `PyList_New`/`PyList_Append` raises `MemoryError`), the cleanup `PyMapping_DelItem(modules, info->name)` (L2011) deletes from `sys.modules` with the `MemoryError` still pending; the dict `__delitem__` slot *succeeds*, so the debug `assert(_Py_CheckSlotResult(...))` in `PyObject_DelItem` (`Objects/abstract.c:280`) fires the fatal 'slot succeeded with an exception set' invariant and aborts
notes: Fatal _Py_CheckSlotResult invariant: 'Slot __delitem__ of type dict succeeded with an exception set'. Triggered when reload_singlephase_extension (import.c) re-imports a single-phase legacy C extension (readline in both vehicles) under OOM: _modules_by_index_set (L2010) raises MemoryError via PyList_New/PyList_Append, then the cleanup PyMapping_DelItem (L2011) deletes the name from sys.modules with the exception still set; the dict delete succeeds, so the debug assert(_Py_CheckSlotResult(o,'__delitem__',res>=0)) at abstract.c:280 aborts. Debug-only: the check sits inside assert(), compiled out under NDEBUG. Matrix on the vehicle source.py: ft_debug_asan -> abort (rc134, authoritative); jit (Py_DEBUG=1, GIL) -> same _Py_CheckSlotResult abort (rc134); ft_release (NDEBUG) -> clean (rc0, stale MemoryError harmless here); upstream (NDEBUG) -> a SEPARATE OOM segfault elsewhere (faulthandler stack at pdb._pyrepl_available -> import-lock _find_and_load), not this site, recorded n/a. Fix: save/restore the pending exception around the cleanup delete (PyErr_GetRaisedException/PyErr_SetRaisedException) so the dict __delitem__ slot does not run with a stale exception. MINIMAL REPRO (shrinkray-reduced from the vehicle, then cleaned; deterministic, re-verified): `pdb.runcall()` under the set_nomemory sweep.
